rulings.Case Title: BRYANT FU ET AL VS. TINA YAN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 27, 2021 line 1. CROSS DEFENDANT STELLA CHEN'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT is continued to September 17, 2021. By no later than September 10, 2021 Cross-Complaint to file a proposed amended complaint in compliance with the Court's ruling on the demurrer to Cross-Complaint with all required factual allegations in bold. Plaintiff may also supplement the facts required by the previous ruling with any facts regarding the non-time barred acts of any of the alleged co-conspirators (time of transfer and asset transfer) in furtherance of the conspiracy. If cross-complainant cannot allege in good faith facts per the Court's prior ruling, demurrer will be sustained without leave to amend. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JAMES D. BANKS VS. WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 27, 2021 line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT JAMES BANKS' DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend. The cause of action for services rendered, as stated in opposition is based on the theory of quantum meruit. Such cause of action requires factual support that "the services were rendered under some understanding or expectation of both parties that compensation therefor was to be made." Cross-complaint currently appears to allege that in exchange for exclusive possession of the premises Banks requested and Courthouse Ventures provided services for which reasonable rental value is being sought. No other services are alleged. In the context of the quantum meruit claim this appears to be an allegation that Banks had exclusive possession and it was the understanding of both parties that compensation would be rendered in exchange, e.g. rental agreement between the parties? Courthouse Ventures shall have a leave to amend to allege what services were rendered to Banks and what was the understanding/expectation of both parties regarding compensation. If Courthouse Ventures is in fact alleging some form of implied rental agreement (combination of current allegations in the complaint and reference to quantum meruit theory in opposition at 3-17) Ventures shall so allege. Courthouse Ventures shall also only allege a claim for damages for reasonable rental value that is not already made in the pending unlawful detainer in which reasonable rental value is sought for a period from February 27, 2017 to entry of judgment. The cause of action for quiet title appear to be incomplete. Paragraph 18 stops in mid-sentence suggesting that Courthouse Ventures intended to include the date of determination of quiet title. Courthouse Ventures shall have leave to clarify. Furthermore, Courthouse Ventures claims to be an equitable owner of the property and asserts that Banks claims and interest adverse to Courthouse Ventures. The Court of Appeals in its September 11, 2020 Order in this action stated: "A quiet title cause of action generally has two elements: (1) "the plaintiff is the owner and in possession of the land," and (2) "the defendant claims an interest therein adverse to [the plaintiff]." Courthouse Ventures shall have leave to amend to allege facts to support both elements or to allege facts supporting any exception to the general rule. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Vacate Orders Granting Plaintiffs April 22, 2019 Ex Parte Application Effectively Denying The Borrowers Statutory Right Of Redemption As Void And Contrary To Law
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 27, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT XELAN PROP 1, LLC'S NOTICE OF MOTION TO VACATE ORDERS GRANTING PLAINTIFFS APRIL 22, 2019 EX PARTE APPLICATION EFFECTIVELY DENYING THE BORROWERS STATUTORY RIGHT OF REDEMPTION AS VOID AND CONTRARY TO LAW is DENIED. Nothing of the face of the "judgment roll" indicates that April 22, 2019 order is void. The order concerned an unauthorized encumbrance in violation of a Court order; determination regarding stay requirements on appeal and authorization to obtain counsel. The order did not make determinations regarding right of redemption which is purportedly the basis for the moving party's argument that the order is void. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: UMPQUA BANK VS. XELAN PROP 1, LLC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Vacate April 22, 2019 Ex Parte Order On Grounds That The Motion Was Procedurally Defective Resulting In A Void Order
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 27, 2021 line 4. DEFENDANT XELAN PROP 1, LLC'S MOTION TO VACATE APRIL 22, 2019 EX PARTE ORDER ON GROUNDS THAT THE MOTION WAS PROCEDURALLY DEFECTIVE RESULTING IN A VOID ORDER is DENIED. There is nothing on the face of the "judgment roll" indicating that the judgment is void. On April 19, 2019 the ex-parte application was submitted for filing. The ex-parte calendar starts at 9:00 a.m. in the morning and parties are required to provide courtesy copies of their filings in support and in opposition (if any) to the Court. The Court hears the applications and reviews the courtesy copies. Apparently also on April 22, 2019 a clerk of the Court reviewed and rejected the entirety of the filing due to formatting issues because a cover page was missing on the exhibits submitted separately for filing from the application itself. The formatting error was rectified in the re-submitted application and it appears on file with a May 7, 2019 filing date. There is no dispute that the application was submitted for filing and copies were provided to the Court and the parties. All other arguments brought in this motion sound like a motion for reconsideration and do not support finding of voidness (e.g. "the key declarations, were riddled with evidentiary insufficiencies, as the declarations were conclusory, based upon hearsay, and layered with suppositions."). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. rulings.Case Title: JONES MEMORIAL HOMES, INC. VS. MARK ANTHONY SMITH
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 27, 2021 line 10. DEFENDANT MARK SMITH'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts to support each element of a cause of action. On its face, Complaint appears to attempt to allege a cause of action for forcible detainer, but is missing factual support on each element per CCP 1160. Opposition appears to suggest that a third party, "Unity Homes" is also asserting a cause of action for forcible detainer against the defendant for possession of 1418 Kirkwood Avenue, San Francisco, California 94124, which is not the premises at issue in this case per paragraph 3 of the Complaint. Opposition further appears to suggest that Plaintiff is asserting a cause for action based on just cause of action under SFRO. No such allegations appear in the face of the Complaint. If Plaintiff can allege in good faith either a cause of action for forcible detainer and/or unlawful detainer (facts to support each element of each cause of action) against the defendant for possession of premises in the City and County of San Francisco, Plaintiff shall have 5-day leave to amend. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed order tracking this ruling a present it for execution at the time of the hearing. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
